Highly Qualified Teacher, as added by , , ; amended by , , ; , , ; , , , renumbered div. B, , , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–242, § 163Pub. L. 111–322, title I, § 1(a)(2)124 Stat. 3521Pub. L. 112–175, § 145126 Stat. 1322Pub. L. 113–46, div. A, § 144127 Stat. 565Pub. L. 114–53, div. B, § 140129 Stat. 510Pub. L. 114–113, § 8(3)129 Stat. 2245Dec. 22, 2010Sept. 28, 2012Oct. 17, 2013Sept. 30, 2015Dec. 18, 2015“(a)A ‘highly qualified teacher’ includes a teacher who meets the requirements in 34 CFR 200.56(a)(2)(ii), as published in the Federal Register on .December 2, 2002“(b)This provision is effective on the date of enactment of this provision [] through the end of the 2016–2017 academic year.Dec. 22, 2010“(c)Not later than , the Secretary of Education shall submit a report to the Committees on Appropriations and Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committees on Appropriations and Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives, using data required under existing law ( [probably meant , as added by ; former ]) by State and each local educational agency, regarding the extent to which students in the following categories are taught by teachers who are deemed highly qualified pursuant to 34 CFR 200.56(a)(2)(ii) as published in the Federal Register on :December 31, 2013December 2, 2002section 1111(h)(6)(A) of Public Law 107–110section 1111(h)(6)(A) of Pub. L. 89–10Pub. L. 107–11020 U.S.C. 6311(h)(6)(A)“(1)Students with disabilities.“(2)English Learners.“(3)Students in rural areas.“(4)Students from low-income families.”

L. 89–10Pub. L. 107–11020 U.S.C. 6311(h)(6)(A)“(1)Students with disabilities.“(2)English Learners.“(3)Students in rural areas.“(4)Students from low-income families.”

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate number of days of attendance of all students during a school year; divided by

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of days school is in session during that year.

children.heading: Conversion
children.label: B Conversion
children.text: The Secretary shall permit the conversion of average daily membership (or other similar data) to average daily attendance for local educational agencies in States that provide State aid to local educational agencies on the basis of average daily membership (or other similar data).

children.label: i
children.text: consider the child to be in attendance at a school of the agency making the payment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not consider the child to be in attendance at a school of the agency receiving the payment.

children.heading: Children with disabilities
children.label: D Children with disabilities
children.text: If a local educational agency makes a tuition payment to a private school or to a public school of another local educational agency for a child with a disability, as defined in , the Secretary shall, for the purpose of this chapter, consider the child to be in attendance at a school of the agency making the payment.section 1401 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate current expenditures, during the third fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which the determination is made (or, if satisfactory data for that year are not available, during the most recent preceding fiscal year for which satisfactory data are available) of all local educational agencies in the State or, in the case of the United States, for all States (which, for the purpose of this paragraph, means the 50 States and the District of Columbia); plus

children.label: ii
children.text: any direct current expenditures by the State for the operation of those agencies; divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate number of children in average daily attendance to whom those agencies provided free public education during that preceding year.

children.heading: Child
children.label: 3 Child
children.text: The term “child” means any person within the age limits for which the State provides free public education.

children.heading: Child with a disability
children.label: 4 Child with a disability
children.text: The term “child with a disability” has the same meaning given that term in .section 1401 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is representative of a community or significant segments of a community; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides educational or related services to individuals in the community.

children.heading: Consolidated local application
children.label: 6 Consolidated local application
children.text: The term “consolidated local application” means an application submitted by a local educational agency pursuant to .section 7845 of this title

children.heading: Consolidated local plan
children.label: 7 Consolidated local plan
children.text: The term “consolidated local plan” means a plan submitted by a local educational agency pursuant to .section 7845 of this title

children.heading: Consolidated State application
children.label: 8 Consolidated State application
children.text: The term “consolidated State application” means an application submitted by a State educational agency pursuant to .section 7842 of this title

children.heading: Consolidated State plan
children.label: 9 Consolidated State plan
children.text: The term “consolidated State plan” means a plan submitted by a State educational agency pursuant to .section 7842 of this title

children.heading: County
children.label: 10 County
children.text: The term “county” means one of the divisions of a State used by the Secretary of Commerce in compiling and reporting data regarding counties.

children.label: A
children.text: part A of subchapter I;

children.label: B
children.text: part C of subchapter I;

children.label: C
children.text: part D of subchapter I;

children.label: D
children.text: part A of subchapter II;

children.label: E
children.text: part A of subchapter III;

children.label: F
children.text: part A of subchapter IV;

children.label: G
children.text: part B of subchapter IV; and

children.label: H
children.text: subpart 2 of part B of subchapter V.

children.label: A
children.text: including expenditures for administration, instruction, attendance and health services, pupil transportation services, operation and maintenance of plant, fixed charges, and net expenditures to cover deficits for food services and student body activities; but

children.label: B
children.text: not including expenditures for community services, capital outlay, and debt service, or any expenditures made from funds received under subchapter I.

children.heading: Department
children.label: 13 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Education.

children.heading: Distance learning
children.label: 14 Distance learning
children.text: The term “distance learning” means the transmission of educational or instructional programming to geographically dispersed individuals and groups via telecommunications.

children.label: A
children.text: is transferable to the institutions of higher education in the partnership; and

children.label: B
children.text: applies toward completion of a degree or recognized educational credential as described in the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Early childhood education program
children.label: 16 Early childhood education program
children.text: The term “early childhood education program” has the meaning given the term in section 103 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ().20 U.S.C. 1003

children.heading: Early college high school
children.label: 17 Early college high school
children.text: The term “early college high school” means a partnership between at least one local educational agency and at least one institution of higher education that allows participants to simultaneously complete requirements toward earning a regular high school diploma and earn not less than 12 credits that are transferable to the institutions of higher education in the partnership as part of an organized course of study toward a postsecondary degree or credential at no cost to the participant or participant’s family.

children.heading: Educational service agency
children.label: 18 Educational service agency
children.text: The term “educational service agency” means a regional public multiservice agency authorized by State statute to develop, manage, and provide services or programs to local educational agencies.

children.heading: Elementary school
children.label: 19 Elementary school
children.text: The term “elementary school” means a nonprofit institutional day or residential school, including a public elementary charter school, that provides elementary education, as determined under State law.

children.label: A
children.text: who is aged 3 through 21;

children.label: B
children.text: who is enrolled or preparing to enroll in an elementary school or secondary school;

children.label: i
children.text: who was not born in the United States or whose native language is a language other than English;

children.label: I
children.text: who is a Native American or Alaska Native, or a native resident of the outlying areas; and

children.label: II
children.text: who comes from an environment where a language other than English has had a significant impact on the individual’s level of English language proficiency; or

children.label: iii
children.text: who is migratory, whose native language is a language other than English, and who comes from an environment where a language other than English is dominant; and

children.label: i
children.text: the ability to meet the challenging State academic standards;

children.label: ii
children.text: the ability to successfully achieve in classrooms where the language of instruction is English; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the opportunity to participate fully in society.

children.label: I
children.text: strong evidence from at least 1 well-designed and well-implemented experimental study;

children.label: II
children.text: moderate evidence from at least 1 well-designed and well-implemented quasi-experimental study; or

children.label: III
children.text: promising evidence from at least 1 well-designed and well-implemented correlational study with statistical controls for selection bias; or

children.label: I
children.text: demonstrates a rationale based on high-quality research findings or positive evaluation that such activity, strategy, or intervention is likely to improve student outcomes or other relevant outcomes; and

children.label: II
children.text: includes ongoing efforts to examine the effects of such activity, strategy, or intervention.

children.heading: Definition for specific activities funded under this chapter
children.label: B Definition for specific activities funded under this chapter
children.text: When used with respect to interventions or improvement activities or strategies funded under , the term “evidence-based” means a State, local educational agency, or school activity, strategy, or intervention that meets the requirements of subclause (I), (II), or (III) of subparagraph (A)(i).section 6303 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: activities and instruction for enrichment as part of a well-rounded education; and

children.label: B
children.text: instructional and support staff to collaborate, plan, and engage in professional development (including professional development on family and community engagement) within and across grades and subjects.

children.label: I
children.text: adding the students who joined that cohort, after the date of the determination of the original cohort; and

children.label: II
children.text: subtracting only those students who left that cohort, after the date of the determination of the original cohort, as described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: AA
children.text: one or more additional years beyond the fourth year of high school; or

children.label: BB
children.text: a summer session immediately following the additional year of high school; and

children.label: AA
children.text: standards-based;

children.label: BB
children.text: aligned with the State requirements for the regular high school diploma; and

children.label: CC
children.text: obtained within the time period for which the State ensures the availability of a free appropriate public education under ; andsection 1412(a)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: shall not include any student awarded a recognized equivalent of a diploma, such as a general equivalency diploma, certificate of completion, certificate of attendance, or similar lesser credential.

children.heading: Cohort removal
children.label: B Cohort removal
children.text: To remove a student from a cohort, a school or local educational agency shall require documentation, or obtain documentation from the State educational agency, to confirm that the student has transferred out, emigrated to another country, or transferred to a prison or juvenile facility, or is deceased.

children.heading: Transferred out
children.label: C Transferred out
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “transferred out” has the meaning given the term in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of paragraph (25)(C).

children.heading: Schools starting after grade 9
children.label: i Schools starting after grade 9
children.text: For those high schools that start after grade 9, the original cohort shall be calculated for the earliest high school grade students attend no later than the date by which student membership data is collected annually by State educational agencies for submission to the National Center for Education Statistics pursuant to .section 9543 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: averaging the extended-year adjusted cohort graduation rate of the school over a period of three years; or

children.label: II
children.text: establishing a minimum number of students that must be included in the cohort described in clause (i) of subparagraph (A) that will provide a valid graduation rate calculation as determined by the Secretary, below which the school shall be exempt from differentiation and identification under such section.

children.label: A
children.text: Interactive literacy activities between parents and their children.

children.label: B
children.text: Training for parents regarding how to be the primary teacher for their children and full partners in the education of their children.

children.label: C
children.text: Parent literacy training that leads to economic self-sufficiency.

children.label: D
children.text: An age-appropriate education to prepare children for success in school and life experiences.

children.label: I
children.text: adding the students who joined that cohort, after the date of the determination of the original cohort; and

children.label: II
children.text: subtracting only those students who left that cohort, after the date of the determination of the original cohort, as described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: AA
children.text: the fourth year of high school; or

children.label: BB
children.text: a summer session immediately following the fourth year of high school; and

children.label: AA
children.text: standards-based;

children.label: BB
children.text: aligned with the State requirements for the regular high school diploma; and

children.label: CC
children.text: obtained within the time period for which the State ensures the availability of a free appropriate public education under ; andsection 1412(a)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: shall not include any student awarded a recognized equivalent of a diploma, such as a general equivalency diploma, certificate of completion, certificate of attendance, or similar lesser credential.

children.heading: Cohort removal
children.label: B Cohort removal
children.text: To remove a student from a cohort, a school or local educational agency shall require documentation, or obtain documentation from the State educational agency, to confirm that the student has transferred out, emigrated to another country, or transferred to a prison or juvenile facility, or is deceased.

children.label: I
children.text: another school from which the student is expected to receive a regular high school diploma; or

children.label: II
children.text: another educational program from which the student is expected to receive a regular high school diploma or an alternate diploma that meets the requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii)(I)(bb).

children.heading: Documentation required
children.label: I Documentation required
children.text: The confirmation of a student’s transfer to another school or educational program described in clause (i) requires documentation of such transfer from the receiving school or program in which the student enrolled.

children.heading: Lack of confirmation
children.label: II Lack of confirmation
children.text: A student who was enrolled in a high school, but for whom there is no confirmation of the student having transferred out, shall remain in the adjusted cohort.

children.heading: Programs not providing credit
children.label: iii Programs not providing credit
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I)(bb), a student who is retained in grade or who is enrolled in a program leading to a general equivalency diploma, or other alternative educational program that does not issue or provide credit toward the issuance of a regular high school diploma, shall not be considered transferred out and shall remain in the adjusted cohort.

children.heading: Schools starting after grade 9
children.label: i Schools starting after grade 9
children.text: For those high schools that start after grade 9, the original cohort shall be calculated for the earliest high school grade students attend no later than the date by which student membership data must be collected annually by State educational agencies for submission to the National Center for Education Statistics pursuant to .section 9543 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: averaging the four-year adjusted cohort graduation rate of the school over a period of three years; or

children.label: II
children.text: establishing a minimum number of students that must be included in the cohort described in clause (i) of subparagraph (A) that will provide a valid graduation rate calculation as determined by the Secretary, below which the school shall be exempt from differentiation and identification under such section.

children.label: A
children.text: at public expense, under public supervision and direction, and without tuition charge; and

children.label: B
children.text: as elementary school or secondary school education as determined under applicable State law, except that the term does not include any education provided beyond grade 12.

children.heading: Gifted and talented
children.label: 27 Gifted and talented
children.text: The term “gifted and talented”, when used with respect to students, children, or youth, means students, children, or youth who give evidence of high achievement capability in areas such as intellectual, creative, artistic, or leadership capacity, or in specific academic fields, and who need services or activities not ordinarily provided by the school in order to fully develop those capabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: grants a diploma, as defined by the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes, at least, grade 12.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 29 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given that term in .section 1001(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “local educational agency” means a public board of education or other public authority legally constituted within a State for either administrative control or direction of, or to perform a service function for, public elementary schools or secondary schools in a city, county, township, school district, or other political subdivision of a State, or of or for a combination of school districts or counties that is recognized in a State as an administrative agency for its public elementary schools or secondary schools.

children.heading: Administrative control and direction
children.label: B Administrative control and direction
children.text: The term includes any other public institution or agency having administrative control and direction of a public elementary school or secondary school.

children.heading: Bureau of Indian Education schools
children.label: C Bureau of Indian Education schools
children.text: The term includes an elementary school or secondary school funded by the Bureau of Indian Education but only to the extent that including the school makes the school eligible for programs for which specific eligibility is not provided to the school in another provision of law and the school does not have a student population that is smaller than the student population of the local educational agency receiving assistance under this chapter with the smallest student population, except that the school shall not be subject to the jurisdiction of any State educational agency other than the Bureau of Indian Education.

children.heading: Educational service agencies
children.label: D Educational service agencies
children.text: The term includes educational service agencies and consortia of those agencies.

children.heading: State educational agency
children.label: E State educational agency
children.text: The term includes the State educational agency in a State in which the State educational agency is the sole educational agency for all public schools.

children.heading: Mentoring
children.label: 31 Mentoring
children.text: The term “mentoring”, except when used to refer to teacher mentoring, means a process by which a responsible adult, postsecondary student, or secondary school student works with a child to provide a positive role model for the child, to establish a supportive relationship with the child, and to provide the child with academic assistance and exposure to new experiences and examples of opportunity that enhance the ability of the child to become a responsible adult.

children.heading: Middle grades
children.label: 32 Middle grades
children.text: The term middle grades means any of grades 5 through 8.

children.heading: Multi-tier system of supports
children.label: 33 Multi-tier system of supports
children.text: The term “multi-tier system of supports” means a comprehensive continuum of evidence-based, systemic practices to support a rapid response to students’ needs, with regular observation to facilitate data-based instructional decisionmaking.

children.heading: Native American and Native American language
children.label: 34 Native American and Native American language
children.text: The terms “Native American” and “Native American language” have the same meaning given those terms in .section 2902 of title 25

children.heading: Other staff
children.label: 35 Other staff
children.text: The term “other staff” means specialized instructional support personnel, librarians, career guidance and counseling personnel, education aides, and other instructional and administrative personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: means American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, and the United States Virgin Islands; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the purpose of any discretionary grant program under this chapter, includes the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, and the Republic of Palau, to the extent that any such grant program continues to be available to State and local governments in the United States.

children.heading: Paraprofessional
children.label: 37 Paraprofessional
children.text: The term “paraprofessional”, also known as a “paraeducator”, includes an education assistant and instructional assistant.

children.heading: Parent
children.label: 38 Parent
children.text: The term “parent” includes a legal guardian or other person standing in loco parentis (such as a grandparent or stepparent with whom the child lives, or a person who is legally responsible for the child’s welfare).

children.label: A
children.text: that parents play an integral role in assisting their child’s learning;

children.label: B
children.text: that parents are encouraged to be actively involved in their child’s education at school;

children.label: C
children.text: that parents are full partners in their child’s education and are included, as appropriate, in decisionmaking and on advisory committees to assist in the education of their child; and

children.label: D
children.text: the carrying out of other activities, such as those described in .section 6318 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a feasibility study on the initiative describing how the proposed intervention is based on evidence of effectiveness;

children.label: B
children.text: a rigorous, third-party evaluation that uses experimental or quasi-experimental design or other research methodologies that allow for the strongest possible causal inferences to determine whether the initiative has met its proposed outcomes;

children.label: C
children.text: an annual, publicly available report on the progress of the initiative; and

children.label: D
children.text: a requirement that payments are made to the recipient of a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement only when agreed upon outcomes are achieved, except that the entity may make payments to the third party conducting the evaluation described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Poverty line
children.label: 41 Poverty line
children.text: The term “poverty line” means the poverty line (as defined by the Office of Management and Budget and revised annually in accordance with ) applicable to a family of the size involved.section 9902(2) of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: are an integral part of school and local educational agency strategies for providing educators (including teachers, principals, other school leaders, specialized instructional support personnel, paraprofessionals, and, as applicable, early childhood educators) with the knowledge and skills necessary to enable students to succeed in a well-rounded education and to meet the challenging State academic standards; and

children.label: I
children.text: knowledge of the academic subjects the teachers teach;

children.label: II
children.text: understanding of how students learn; and

children.label: III
children.text: ability to analyze student work and achievement from multiple sources, including how to adjust instructional strategies, assessments, and materials based on such analysis;

children.label: ii
children.text: are an integral part of broad schoolwide and districtwide educational improvement plans;

children.label: iii
children.text: allow personalized plans for each educator to address the educator’s specific needs identified in observation or other feedback;

children.label: iv
children.text: improve classroom management skills;

children.label: v
children.text: support the recruitment, hiring, and training of effective teachers, including teachers who became certified through State and local alternative routes to certification;

children.label: I
children.text: effective instructional strategies that are evidence-based; and

children.label: II
children.text: strategies for improving student academic achievement or substantially increasing the knowledge and teaching skills of teachers;

children.label: vii
children.text: are aligned with, and directly related to, academic goals of the school or local educational agency;

children.label: viii
children.text: are developed with extensive participation of teachers, principals, other school leaders, parents, representatives of Indian tribes (as applicable), and administrators of schools to be served under this chapter;

children.label: ix
children.text: are designed to give teachers of English learners, and other teachers and instructional staff, the knowledge and skills to provide instruction and appropriate language and academic support services to those children, including the appropriate use of curricula and assessments;

children.label: x
children.text: to the extent appropriate, provide training for teachers, principals, and other school leaders in the use of technology (including education about the harms of copyright piracy), so that technology and technology applications are effectively used in the classroom to improve teaching and learning in the curricula and academic subjects in which the teachers teach;

children.label: xi
children.text: as a whole, are regularly evaluated for their impact on increased teacher effectiveness and improved student academic achievement, with the findings of the evaluations used to improve the quality of professional development;

children.label: xii
children.text: are designed to give teachers of children with disabilities or children with developmental delays, and other teachers and instructional staff, the knowledge and skills to provide instruction and academic support services, to those children, including positive behavioral interventions and supports, multi-tier system of supports, and use of accommodations;

children.label: xiii
children.text: include instruction in the use of data and assessments to inform and instruct classroom practice;

children.label: xiv
children.text: include instruction in ways that teachers, principals, other school leaders, specialized instructional support personnel, and school administrators may work more effectively with parents and families;

children.label: xv
children.text: involve the forming of partnerships with institutions of higher education, including, as applicable, Tribal Colleges and Universities as defined in section 316(b) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (), to establish school-based teacher, principal, and other school leader training programs that provide prospective teachers, novice teachers, principals, and other school leaders with an opportunity to work under the guidance of experienced teachers, principals, other school leaders, and faculty of such institutions;20 U.S.C. 1059c(b)

children.label: xvi
children.text: create programs to enable paraprofessionals (assisting teachers employed by a local educational agency receiving assistance under part A of subchapter I) to obtain the education necessary for those paraprofessionals to become certified and licensed teachers;

children.label: xvii
children.text: provide follow-up training to teachers who have participated in activities described in this paragraph that are designed to ensure that the knowledge and skills learned by the teachers are implemented in the classroom; and

children.label: xviii
children.text: where practicable, provide jointly for school staff and other early childhood education program providers, to address the transition to elementary school, including issues related to school readiness.

children.label: A
children.text: means the standard high school diploma awarded to the preponderance of students in the State that is fully aligned with State standards, or a higher diploma, except that a regular high school diploma shall not be aligned to the alternate academic achievement standards described in ; andsection 6311(b)(1)(E) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a recognized equivalent of a diploma, such as a general equivalency diploma, certificate of completion, certificate of attendance, or similar lesser credential.

children.label: A
children.text: an employee or officer of an elementary school or secondary school, local educational agency, or other entity operating an elementary school or secondary school; and

children.label: B
children.text: responsible for the daily instructional leadership and managerial operations in the elementary school or secondary school building.

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: 45 Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” means a nonprofit institutional day or residential school, including a public secondary charter school, that provides secondary education, as determined under State law, except that the term does not include any education beyond grade 12.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 46 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Education.

children.heading: Specialized instructional support personnel; specialized instructional support services
children.label: 47 Specialized instructional support personnel; specialized instructional support services
i
children.text: school counselors, school social workers, and school psychologists; and

children.label: ii
children.text: other qualified professional personnel, such as school nurses, speech language pathologists, and school librarians, involved in providing assessment, diagnosis, counseling, educational, therapeutic, and other necessary services (including related services as that term is defined in ) as part of a comprehensive program to meet student needs.section 1401 of this title

children.heading: Specialized instructional support services
children.label: B Specialized instructional support services
children.text: The term “specialized instructional support services” means the services provided by specialized instructional support personnel.

children.heading: State
children.label: 48 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and each of the outlying areas.

children.heading: State educational agency
children.label: 49 State educational agency
children.text: The term “State educational agency” means the agency primarily responsible for the State supervision of public elementary schools and secondary schools.

children.heading: Technology
children.label: 50 Technology
children.text: The term “technology” means modern information, computer and communication technology products, services, or tools, including, the Internet and other communications networks, computer devices and other computer and communications hardware, software applications, data systems, and other electronic content (including multimedia content) and data storage.

children.heading: Universal design for learning
children.label: 51 Universal design for learning
children.text: The term “universal design for learning” has the meaning given the term in section 103 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ().20 U.S.C. 1003

children.heading: Well-rounded education
children.label: 52 Well-rounded education
children.text: The term “well-rounded education” means courses, activities, and programming in subjects such as English, reading or language arts, writing, science, technology, engineering, mathematics, foreign languages, civics and government, economics, arts, history, geography, computer science, music, career and technical education, health, physical education, and any other subject, as determined by the State or local educational agency, with the purpose of providing all students access to an enriched curriculum and educational experience.
